description_long: This object is a vintage television set housed in a rectangular wooden cabinet with four tapered legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The front features a large, convex glass screen set within a dark bezel, flanked by a fabric-covered speaker grille on the left and a control panel with several knobs and buttons below the screen. The wood grain finish is prominent on the sides and top, lending a warm, classic look. The back panel is made of a lighter, perforated material with a protruding circular housing for the cathode ray tube and several ventilation holes. The overall build is robust, with a combination of wood, metal, glass, and fabric materials. This television would have been a common sight in urban homes or public spaces during the mid-20th century.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, fabric speaker grille, and control knobs, standing on four tapered legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, fabric speaker grille, and control knobs below the screen.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, continuous wood grain finish with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the perforated back panel, circular cathode ray tube housing, and ventilation holes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the uninterrupted wood grain surface.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, dark-colored, wood-framed bezel with a classic rectangular shape
attribute_control_button_layout: row of knobs and buttons below the screen on the front panel
